summary: Hungarian author. Known for The Door (about a writer's complex friendship with housekeeper Emerence), Katalin Street, and Abigél. Spent the 1950s teaching after being labeled an enemy of the party.

comprehensive_summary: Magda Szabó (1917-2007) was a Hungarian author who spent the 1950s teaching at a girls' school after being labeled an enemy of the party. Her novel The Door, about a writer's friendship with her unconventional housekeeper Emerence, was republished in Bernard Rix's translation in 2015. Katalin Street follows children playing Cherry Tree before WWII; one character, Henriette Weld, is shot by Nazis and observes her friends as a ghost. She also wrote the young adult novel Abigél.
